name: Smoke Roasted Top Round of Bison
recipeIngredient: 3 chunks hardwood or fruitwood, soaked overnight
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix all wet ingredients (beer dark variety, soy sauce, balsamic vinegar, garlic, Worcestershire sauce, and hot sauce) with dry ingredients (hardwood or fruitwood chunks).
2. Add the Buffalo to the bowl and cover. Refrigerate for 72 hours.
3. Remove the Buffalo from the refrigerator and pat dry with paper towels.
4. Prepare the grill by placing charcoal in a pyramid shape and lighting it using lighter fluid, an electric starter, or a chimney starter.
5. When the coals turn gray, place them on one side of the grill. Add 12-15 additional briquettes to the hot coals on the other side.
6. Place an aluminum pan filled with hot water next to the coals.
7. Spray the grill grid with a non-stick spray and heat over the hot coals.
8. Place the Buffalo above the water pan on the heated grid, adding one wood chunk.
9. Cover the grill, leaving the bottom vents 3/4 open and the top cover vent 1/2 open.
10. Add lit charcoal to the hot coals every hour (approximately 8-12 briquettes) and another wood chunk if desired.
11. Cook for approximately 2 ¼ hours or 45 minutes per pound, maintaining a cooking temperature of about 275 degrees.
12. Let the Buffalo rest for 20 minutes before slicing.
